For New LPers... What does TVL mean?

Does being in a pool early give you more profit?

TVL means Total Value Locked. So essentially it just means the total dollar value $ that is deposited within the pool.

I want you guys to start looking to see how much SOL vs Token is in the pool. This shouldn't really impact your decisions (usually), but I want you to start seeing if there is more Sol in the pool (buyside liquidity) or more tokens in the pool (sell side liquidity)

NOW what does all of this mean. Basically, if a token fits your pooling criteria and you LIKE the token, the amount of TVL really shouldn't impact your decision. TVL value is usually never going to impact my decision making.

HOWEVER it is important to note that when you are early in a pool, or some of the only liquidity, you do have an opportunity to take a majority of the fees that come thru the pool.

With popular tokens doing a lot of volume, people have been getting wayyy quicker to get into pools. EX. $tjr token had a lot of liq deposited very quickly. That shouldn't deter you from getting in.

In fact, sometimes a large amount of TVL will mean the Meteora pool will dominate and receive more orderflow. But I digress.

Also important to note that just because a pool has a TON of buyside liquidity (acts as support for the token) does NOT mean it is safe. I have seen 300k eaten through in no time at all. TVL of a pool ~300k drops to like 30k in a span of an hour. Rugs can still happen.

But, like I said in the clip, having buyside liquidity does provide support for the token if ppl dont absolutely nuke it -80%.

Anyways, long rant, short clip, hopefully that clears it up for you if you are just starting to LP.

Good question from the last stream...

"If you funded the $ansem LP at 1m, would you have printed?"

My answer was kind of ambigous... check out the clip to see how you could have played it.

Since this token was moving pretty straight up, you would have to continue chasing price up with more buy-side LPs (would be my strategy).

You CAN use sell side pools (or two side pools), but tbh in this case you would be better off accumulating fees (imo)

I think this example should make sense to ppl. Continue to chase price up as it appreciates.

DLMMing in a HOT market https://x.com/i/broadcasts/1qJDzzyLRgEKV

How does Liquidity work?

A term that is thrown around a lot, but seems like there is still a good percentage of people who don't understand.

This is a long clip, but if there is one thing you take from it, it is that

Market Orders move price up and down.
Limit Orders stabilize price. Large levels of limit orders make it HARDER for market orders to eat through levels.

Market Orders = Taking liquidity
Limit Orders = Providing liquidity

@MeteoraAG @met_lparmy

The best way to learn is by DOING. 

If you have never opened an LP position, here is a step-by-step cheat sheet so you can get started.

I intentionally made this guide so that you are opening a low-risk DLMM setup when you are creating your first one. You can tweak your shape/range later. Stick with this one while you are learning.

I want you to see how this works, you will gain a ton of understanding by just going ahead and doing it. 

THEN you can get fancy, go tighter, try out different curves.

You don't have to understand everything right now. You will learn as you go. I will be releasing more detailed content on Liquidity, Token Selection, and Risk Management.
